title: 11 Herbal Essential Oils For Coughing Fits
intro_desc: Herbal essential oils can be a big help if you're struggling with coughing fits. These oils are made from plants and are often used in aromatherapy to ease congestion and calm a sore throat. One popular herb that works well is Eucalyptus globulus. This oil has a decongestant effect, which means it helps to break down mucus and clear your airways. When you inhale eucalyptus oil, it can help to ease your cough and make it easier to breathe.  Another herb that can help with coughing is Echinacea purpurea. This plant has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to reduce swelling in your throat and ease your cough. It's also a natural immune system booster, which can help to fight off infections that can cause coughing. You can use echinacea oil in a diffuser or add it to a warm bath to help soothe your throat.  Ginger, or Zingiber officinale, is another herb that can help with coughing. The essential oil from ginger has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to reduce swelling in your throat and ease your cough. It also has a soothing effect, which can help to calm your throat and make it easier to swallow.  Herbal teas made from these herbs can also be a big help when you're struggling with coughing fits. When you drink a warm tea, the steam can help to ease your congestion and calm your throat. The herbs in the tea can also help to soothe your throat and reduce inflammation. Drinking a warm tea can be especially helpful when you're feeling tired or exhausted from coughing. It can help to give you the energy you need to get through the day and feel more comfortable.  One benefit of using herbal essential oils and teas to relieve coughing fits is that they're a natural and chemical-free way to ease your symptoms. This can be especially helpful if you're sensitive to medications or want to avoid harsh chemicals in your body. These oils and teas can also be a more cost-effective way to manage your symptoms, especially if you're using them regularly.
intro_image_alt: herbal essential oils for coughing fits

remedies.remedy_desc: Eucalyptus globulus essential oils contains compounds like eucalyptol, limonene, and pinene, which have decongestant and expectorant properties. Eucalyptol, in particular, helps to loosen and clear mucus from the airways, making it easier to breathe. These properties make Eucalyptus globulus essential oils effective at relieving coughing fits caused by congestion and colds. The decongestant properties of the oil also help to reduce inflammation in the nasal passages and sinuses, further alleviating coughing. By reducing congestion and inflammation, Eucalyptus globulus essential oils helps to provide quick relief from coughing fits.
remedies.image_alt: Eucalyptus globulus herbal essential oils for coughing fits
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh Eucalyptus globulus leaves and 2 cups of water in a large pot.
Add 2 tablespoons of carrier oil (coconut or olive oil) to the pot.
Heat the mixture over low heat for 30 minutes, allowing the leaves to infuse their oils into the water and carrier oil.
Remove the pot from heat and let it cool. Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a separate container.
Store the Eucalyptus globulus essential oil in a cool, dark place. Use 5-7 drops of the oil in a diffuser or mix with a carrier oil for direct application.

remedies.remedy_desc: Echinacea purpurea essential oils contains a high concentration of sesquiterpenes and alkylamides, which are biologically active constituents that help ease respiratory issues. These compounds work by reducing inflammation and congestion in the airways, making it easier to breathe and cough up excess mucus. Alkylamides in particular have been shown to have a calming effect on the muscles in the throat and airways, which can help to stop coughing fits. Sesquiterpenes have antimicrobial properties that help fight off infections that can cause coughing, such as the common cold and flu. By using Echinacea purpurea essential oils, you can help to soothe your cough and get relief from the discomfort it causes.
remedies.image_alt: Echinacea purpurea herbal essential oils for coughing fits
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of Echinacea purpurea flowers and leaves, and 2 cups of carrier oil like coconut or olive oil.
Heat a double boiler or a bowl set over a pot of boiling water, and add 1 cup of carrier oil.
Add the Echinacea flowers and leaves to the oil, and let it steep for 2-3 hours or overnight.
Strain the oil through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean container, and discard the solids.
Store the Echinacea essential oil in a dark glass bottle and use it as needed to help alleviate coughing fits.

remedies.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale essential oils contains a compound called gingerol, which is responsible for its medicinal properties. Gingerol and other bioactive constituents, including zingerone and shogaol, have anti-inflammatory properties that help to soothe and calm the respiratory system. The oils' expectorant properties help to loosen and clear mucus from the lungs, making it easier to cough up and relieving congestion. Additionally, the oils' warm and spicy properties help to ease chest tightness and coughs, providing relief from respiratory discomfort. By stimulating the digestive system and promoting the removal of mucus, Zingiber officinale essential oils can help to alleviate coughing fits and promote a sense of ease and comfort.
remedies.image_alt: Zingiber officinale herbal essential oils for coughing fits
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of Zingiber officinale roots and 2 cups of water in a saucepan.
Heat the water in the saucepan over medium heat until it starts boiling.
Reduce heat to low and add the Zingiber officinale roots to the water.
Steep for 1-2 hours or until the liquid is reduced to 1 cup. Strain the mixture.
Use a carrier oil like coconut oil and add 10-15 drops of the Zingiber officinale essential oil to it. Use this oil for cough relief.

remedies.remedy_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra essential oils contains bioactive constituents like glycyrrhizin and flavonoids. These compounds have anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties, which can help relieve coughing fits by reducing inflammation in the airways. Glycyrrhizin also has a soothing effect on the throat, making it less irritated and more comfortable. The expectorant properties of glycyrrhizin help loosen and clear mucus from the lungs, making it easier to cough up and breathe. By reducing inflammation and loosening mucus, Glycyrrhiza glabra essential oils can provide relief from coughing fits.
remedies.image_alt: Glycyrrhiza glabra herbal essential oils for coughing fits
remedy_procedure: Gather materials: 100g of dried Glycyrrhiza glabra roots, a glass jar, a cheesecloth, a cup of carrier oil (coconut or olive oil), and a dark glass bottle.
Dry the roots: Place the roots in a single layer on a baking sheet and dry in a low oven (150°F - 200°F) for 2-3 hours.
Make the infusion: Fill the glass jar with 100g of dried roots and add 2 cups of carrier oil. Stir and seal the jar.
Steep the roots: Store the jar in a cool, dark place and let it steep for 2-3 weeks, shaking the jar daily.
Filter and bottle: Strain the mixture through cheesecloth into the dark glass bottle. Discard the solids and use the essential oil as needed.

remedies.remedy_desc: Thymus vulgaris essential oils contains thymol, carvacrol, and borneol as its main bioactive constituents. These compounds have antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to soothe and calm the throat and respiratory tract. Thymol, in particular, has been shown to have expectorant properties, helping to loosen and clear mucus from the lungs and airways. Carvacrol, on the other hand, has antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, reducing swelling and pain in the throat and sinuses. By using Thymus vulgaris essential oils, you can help to ease coughing fits and promote a healthy respiratory system.
remedies.image_alt: Thymus vulgaris herbal essential oils for coughing fits
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh Thymus vulgaris leaves and stems, and 2 cups of water.
Wash the Thymus vulgaris leaves and stems in cold water, then gently pat them dry with a paper towel.
Combine the Thymus vulgaris leaves and stems with the 2 cups of water in a medium saucepan.
Heat the mixture over low heat for 30-40 minutes, or until the water has reduced by half and the mixture has a strong aroma.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass bottle, and discard the solids.

remedies.remedy_desc: Mentha x piperita essential oils contains several biologically active constituents, including menthol, menthone, and limonene. These compounds have decongestant and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce swelling in the nasal passages and sinuses. The menthol in peppermint oil, in particular, acts as an expectorant, thinning and loosening mucus, making it easier to cough up. The oil's analgesic and anti-spasmodic properties also help to alleviate coughing fits by reducing irritation and muscle tension in the throat and chest. This combination of properties makes peppermint oil a popular natural remedy for coughing fits and congestion.
remedies.image_alt: Mentha x piperita herbal essential oils for coughing fits
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh Mentha x piperita leaves and 1 cup of water. Clean and wash the leaves.
Combine the leaves and water in a saucepan and bring to a boil.
Reduce heat to medium-low and simmer for 10-15 minutes, allowing the leaves to release their oil.
Remove the saucepan from heat and let it cool for 30 minutes. Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth into a bowl.
Transfer the strained mixture to a dark glass bottle and store it in a cool, dry place. Use 5-7 drops as needed for cough relief.

remedies.remedy_desc: Lavandula angustifolia essential oils contains high levels of linalool and linalyl acetate, two key active constituents that contribute to its therapeutic properties. These constituents help to calm the body and mind, reducing stress and anxiety that can exacerbate coughing fits. The decongestant properties of lavender oil also help to clear airways and ease congestion, making it easier to breathe. Additionally, the anti-inflammatory properties of linalool and linalyl acetate help to soothe the throat and reduce inflammation, providing relief from coughing and soreness. By using Lavandula angustifolia essential oils, you can help to ease coughing fits and promote a sense of calm and relaxation.
remedies.image_alt: Lavandula angustifolia herbal essential oils for coughing fits
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh Lavandula angustifolia flowers and 1 cup of vodka or glycerin in a clean glass jar.
Combine the flowers and vodka or glycerin in the jar. Make sure the flowers are completely covered.
Seal the jar and let it sit in a cool, dark place for 2-3 weeks. Shake the jar daily.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass jar. Discard the solids.
Transfer the liquid to small glass bottles and label them. Store them in a cool, dark place. Use 5-7 drops of the essential oil in a diffuser or mix with a carrier oil for topical use.

remedies.remedy_desc: Sambucus nigra essential oils contains bioactive constituents like flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenes. These compounds have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help soothe and calm the respiratory system. The essential oil's antitussive properties specifically target cough receptors, reducing the frequency and severity of coughing fits. The flavonoids and phenolic acids in Sambucus nigra essential oils also have antimicrobial properties, which help combat underlying infections that can exacerbate coughing. By reducing inflammation and fighting infections, Sambucus nigra essential oils can provide relief from coughing fits.
remedies.image_alt: Sambucus nigra herbal essential oils for coughing fits
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of Sambucus nigra flowers, 2 cups of water, and a glass jar.
Combine the Sambucus nigra flowers and water in the glass jar, then seal it.
Leave the jar in a cool, dark place for 2-3 weeks, shaking it daily.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another container, discarding the solids.
Use a distillation kit or a steam distiller to extract the essential oil from the liquid, then store it in a dark glass bottle.

remedies.remedy_desc: Rosmarinus officinalis essential oils contains carnosic acid, camphor, and bornyl acetate as its active constituents. These compounds have antitussive properties, meaning they help relieve coughs. The camphor in the oil has a cooling effect on the respiratory tract, reducing inflammation and soothing irritated tissues. Bornyl acetate also has expectorant properties, helping to loosen and clear mucus from the airways, making it easier to cough up. By reducing inflammation and loosening mucus, Rosmarinus officinalis essential oils can help alleviate coughing fits.
remedies.image_alt: Rosmarinus officinalis herbal essential oils for coughing fits
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh Rosmarinus officinalis leaves and 2 cups of water in a pot.
Bring the water to a boil, then reduce heat and add the leaves. Let it simmer for 2 hours.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a bowl. Discard the solids.
Transfer the liquid to a dark glass bottle and let it cool. Store it in the fridge.
To use, mix 5-7 drops of the essential oil with 1 tablespoon of honey or water. Take as needed to relieve coughing fits.

remedies.remedy_desc: Cinnamomum verum essential oils contains compounds like cinnamaldehyde and linalool, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. These bioactive constituents have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making them effective in soothing irritated throats and reducing coughing fits. Cinnamomum verum essential oils also has expectorant properties, which help to loosen and clear mucus from the airways, providing relief from coughing and congestion. The warm, spicy aroma of cinnamomum verum essential oils can help to ease respiratory issues by opening up the airways and promoting easier breathing. When inhaled, the essential oil can help to calm the nervous system and reduce stress, which can exacerbate coughing fits.
remedies.image_alt: Cinnamomum verum herbal essential oils for coughing fits
remedy_procedure: Gather 2 cups of Cinnamomum verum bark, 4 cups of water, and a large saucepan.
Combine the Cinnamomum verum bark and water in the saucepan, and heat it on medium heat.
Reduce heat to low and simmer for 2 hours, then let it steep for 2 more hours.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth into a clean container, discarding the solids.
Store the essential oil in a dark glass bottle and use 5-7 drops per day to relieve coughing fits.

remedies.remedy_desc: Salvia officinalis essential oils contains a number of bioactive constituents, including carnosic acid, rosmarinic acid, and ursolic acid. These compounds have anti-inflammatory properties, which help reduce swelling and irritation in the throat and lungs. The antimicrobial properties of Salvia officinalis essential oils also help combat infections that can cause coughing fits. Additionally, the expectorant properties of the oil help loosen and clear mucus from the airways, making it easier to breathe and cough up irritants. By reducing inflammation, fighting infections, and promoting mucus clearance, Salvia officinalis essential oils can help alleviate coughing fits.
remedies.image_alt: Salvia officinalis herbal essential oils for coughing fits
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of Salvia officinalis leaves and 2 cups of water in a saucepan. Heat the water over low heat.
Reduce the heat to medium-low and let the leaves simmer for 30 minutes. Stir occasionally.
Remove the saucepan from the heat and let it cool. Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth into a bowl.
Discard the solids and transfer the liquid to a glass bottle. Add 1 tablespoon of carrier oil like coconut oil.
Seal the bottle and store it in a cool, dark place. Use a few drops of the essential oil in a diffuser or apply topically (after dilution) for cough relief.

faq_prevent: Drinking herbal tea may help soothe a cough. Some teas, like peppermint or ginger, can ease irritation in the throat and calm the body. This can lead to fewer coughing fits. Herbal tea's calming effects can also help reduce stress, which can contribute to coughing. This might give you some relief.
faq_safe: Consuming herbal teas for coughing fits every day can be safe, but it depends on the type of tea and its ingredients. Some teas, like peppermint and ginger, are generally safe in large quantities. However, others, like those containing eucalyptus or licorice root, might be too potent and cause issues if overused.
faq_timeline: Herbal teas can start to help with coughing fits within a few hours. Some people notice relief after an hour or two, while others may take a bit longer, like 3-4 hours. It really depends on the person and the specific tea they're drinking.
faq_time: Drinking herbal tea for coughing fits is best when you're not eating or drinking anything else. Try sipping it during a quiet morning or afternoon, when your body is more likely to calm down. This can help soothe your throat and ease your cough.
faq_interaction: Yes, herbal teas can interact with prescription medications for coughing fits. For example, certain herbs like ginger and ginseng may increase blood thinning effects when taken with medications like warfarin. Other herbs like peppermint and licorice root may also affect blood pressure or interact with cough medicines, so be cautious when combining them with prescription medications.
faq_pregnant: When pregnant, it's a good idea to be cautious with herbal teas. Some, like peppermint and ginger, are usually safe, but others like sage and pennyroyal can be too strong for the baby. If you're experiencing coughing fits, consider sticking to plain water or a weak tea like chamomile, which might help soothe your throat.
faq_breastfeeding: Herbal teas can be a soothing relief for coughs, but be cautious when breastfeeding. Some herbs, like peppermint and sage, are generally safe, but others, like chamomile and licorice root, can pass into breast milk and cause issues in some babies. Choose teas labeled as "breastfeeding-friendly" or check the ingredients carefully.
title_2: Say Goodbye to Coughing Fits with 11 Herbal Essential Oils for Pneumonia Patients
